title: Cirrus: Re-order tasks for more parallelism
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Execute all tasks in three dependent, parallel groups

1. Meta + smoke + vendor + cross
2. Unit + int. + containerized-int. + conformance
3. Success

#### How to verify it

CI will pass in less than 55 minutes

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Ref: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/3699#issuecomment-1010075055

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

Depending on overall CI system load, queuing may reduce the number of parallel jobs actually run.  This aspect is almost entirely outside of our control/influence.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

None

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Observation: Yeah, the second set of tasks are queuing apparently in a group of 10.  So at least one conformance (or other random task) will block.  Getting around this involves either paying Cirrus-CI or removing one task (or matrix element).
Ugh.  Hopefully it's a rare hiccup, but running this way actually took LONGER than the average (by 10 minutes) :disappointed:
